description: Sector overlay for U.S. maritime carriers, vessel operations, ports, domestic shipping, ocean transportation intermediaries, and maritime safety/security documentation.

metadata.scope: United States sector overlay controls for Maritime and Shipping. Intended to be used with us/core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from U.S. federal statutes, regulations, agency guidance, regulator materials, and sector frameworks available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance. State, territorial, tribal, and local obligations may impose additional requirements.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: U.S. Coast Guard maritime regulations and safety/security framework
Federal Maritime Commission licensing and ocean transportation rules
EPA vessel and marine pollution compliance materials

rules.title: Coast Guard vessel documentation, inspection, or safety evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for U.S. Coast Guard documentation, certificate of inspection, vessel safety, or maritime compliance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: U.S. Coast Guard inspection and vessel documentation framework

rules.title: Jones Act/coastwise eligibility evidence is present where domestic carriage is described
rules.summary: Checks for Jones Act, coastwise endorsement, U.S.-built, U.S.-owned, or coastwise-qualified vessel evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: MARAD Jones Act / domestic shipping guidance

rules.title: FMC ocean transportation intermediary or carrier compliance evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for FMC license, ocean transportation intermediary, NVOCC, tariff, service contract, or carrier compliance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Federal Maritime Commission ocean transportation intermediary and carrier materials

rules.title: Marine pollution, discharge, or port environmental controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for ballast, bilge, vessel discharge, oil pollution, spill response, or marine environmental controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Clean Water Act and marine discharge routing where applicable
